rules: Every projection has exactly one P0A normative producer and at least one implementation or acceptance consumer.
Every referenced task exists in this plan generation and mechanically contains every required contract token.
A superseded-plan task result cannot satisfy a v3.23 projection.
Profile generation can select only capabilities jointly admitted by one RuntimeActivationBundle; unsupported capability or architecture changes cannot be enabled by configuration.

global_constraints: Do not create a BaselineApprovalRecord until the normative package is complete and a consistency review is green.
Do not change runtime/host-orchestrator behavior during P0A planning or normative closure tasks.
Do not create runtime/local-ai-runtime before Baseline Approval and the P0B Truth Reset.
Do not claim Implementation Acceptance, Full Q0, P2 admission, live acceptance, or cutover without their durable records.
Treat every work-item result as identified by plan_id plus task_id; a result from a superseded plan cannot close a v3.23 task.

Before P0A closure, Native thin-path / capability comparative evaluation must remain on a fixed snapshot with fixed task families, model/effort, tool inventory, sandbox, gates and human-intervention definition.
CLI, App Server, SDK, managed Worktree and Automations are independent capability surfaces; qualification never transfers across surfaces.
Only preserve_v3_23_semantics releases P0A closure. narrow_profile_or_adapter_candidate or supersede_required freezes v3.23 and requires a successor candidate instead of in-place semantic edits.
Profile generation may only select jointly admitted capabilities; capability generation and architecture epoch changes require their declared acceptance and Q0 paths.
Epoch 1 global capacity remains exactly one; no work item may enable a second writer or same-effect-domain concurrency.
Keep each implementation slice reviewable; five primary files is a target, not a mechanical failure gate.
Keep runtime source closed to approved_root_files and approved_subpackages; package-root files are bootstrap-only and all functional modules belong to approved subpackages.
Preserve user changes and roll back only the current task diff.

new_runtime: uv run --locked --offline --project runtime/local-ai-runtime python -m local_ai_runtime contracts verify
uv run --locked --offline --project runtime/local-ai-runtime ruff check runtime/local-ai-runtime
